messages.content: Loaded mind-context, displayed mindmap, recent context (last session: far_memory schema extensions, stdin mode, staging paths), and memory stats. Session 748b2710 active. Greeted user.
why none of this is there on next starting session ? i did push to remote main...

The files are on disk but the new session's Claude instance still stored a summary in far_memory msg 2 instead of full verbatim. Root cause: CLAUDE.md (the authoritative instruction file) didn't mention the full verbatim rule — it just said '--content2 "assistant response"' which is too vague.

Fixed CLAUDE.md:
1. Added **CRITICAL: far_memory stores FULL VERBATIM content, NEVER summaries** in Every Turn section
2. Documented --stdin mode with heredoc example for large outputs
3. Documented --tools flag for tool call metadata
4. Listed all 7 scripts (was only 4)
5. Added staging paths rule (conventions = self-contained, content = linked to memory)

The next session will read these instructions in CLAUDE.md and follow them.

tools.action: added CRITICAL verbatim rule, stdin mode, tools flag, staging paths to Every Turn
added memory_stats.py, set_depth.py, mindmap_filter.py to scripts list

summaries.summary: Session start: loaded mind-context, displayed mindmap and stats, greeted user
fixed CLAUDE.md: added full verbatim rule, stdin mode docs, tools flag, all 7 scripts, staging paths — ensures next session follows conventions
